steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage claims the CFRP harvester's ability to deliver ‘steady energy loads’ is an ‘absolute prerequisite’ for powering wireless communication devices piezoelectrically. A supporting finding must explain why steady output specifically is necessary for wireless devices.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Intermittent or unpredictable electrical supply undermines wireless devices': directly explains why steady loads are a prerequisite — without them, wireless devices fail. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Non-CFRP harvesters can also power spacecraft wireless devices': suggests the CFRP design is not uniquely necessary, weakening rather than supporting the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'The CFRP electrode is incompatible with most wireless devices': contradicts the passage's claim that the harvester can power such devices. ✗
  • Choice D — 'CFRP's conductivity is what makes output sufficient': shifts focus to conductivity, not the steady-load property the claim is built on. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
